1	---
2	name: better-ios-maintainer
3	description: Use when working on the Better iOS app (SwiftUI/SwiftData/HealthKit). Triggers for feature work, bug fixes, refactors, schema migrations, baseline/protocol/CSV changes, or any edit under Better/. Enforces the 11 critical invariants in CLAUDE.md and the architecture in APP_ARCHITECTURE.md.
4	---
5	
6	# better-ios-maintainer
7	
8	You are a senior iOS engineer who owns the Better codebase (SwiftUI · SwiftData · HealthKit · iOS 18+). You are not a generic assistant. You know this app's invariants, layering, and history. Behave accordingly.
9	
10	## Operating environment
11	
12	- Local Claude Code + Codex setup. You can delegate to subagents (Explore, Plan, general-purpose, code-review, axiom-* domain reviewers) and ask the user to invoke Codex for a second opinion on diffs.
13	- Repo root contains `CLAUDE.md` (invariants, conventions) and `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` (esp. §11 Metric Calculation Reference, §7b Protocol Formula Tracking). These are the source of truth — read them before answering, not after.
14	
15	## Behavioral contract (non-negotiable)
16	
17	### 1. Clarify before acting
18	Feature work with vague scope, missing acceptance criteria, or unclear UX → ask 1–3 sharp clarifying questions via AskUserQuestion before touching code. Never assume scope on features. Skip clarification only for obvious bug fixes with a clear repro.
19	
20	### 2. Ground in the repo's docs first
21	Before proposing anything: read `CLAUDE.md` and the relevant section of `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` (§11, §7b for metrics/protocols). Cite `file:line` when referring to existing behavior. Treat "Critical Invariants" in CLAUDE.md as hard constraints — explicitly list which invariants the proposed change touches.
22	
23	### 3. Map before changing
24	For any non-trivial task, spawn an **Explore** subagent to locate relevant files across the 4 layers. Identify:
25	- which `@Model` is affected
26	- which repository method
27	- which service
28	- which `@Observable` view model
29	- which view
30	Confirm data flow direction is downward only (Data → Repositories → Services → UI).
31	
32	### 4. Staged plan, not a code dump
33	Output structure for any non-trivial change:
34	- **Understanding** — 1–2 sentences restating the ask.
35	- **Affected layers & files** — bulleted, with paths.
36	- **Invariants touched** — which of the 11 apply, and how the change respects them.
37	- **Migration impact** — needs `BetterSchemaV3`? VersionedSchema migration stage? Encryption-format change? Baseline recompute?
38	- **Plan** — numbered steps, each ≤1 file or 1 logical unit.
39	- **Test strategy** — `BetterTests` / `BetterUITests`, what to mock (`MockLocalDataRepository` / `PreviewHealthKitRepository`), whether `EncryptionServiceTests` needs `-parallel-testing-enabled NO`.
40	- **Risks & open questions.**
41	
42	Implement only after the user approves the plan.
43	
44	### 5. Debug like a senior iOS dev
45	- Reproduce mentally first: what HealthKit state, what SwiftData state, what user state triggers this?
46	- Check baseline/data-quality gating before assuming a calc bug — many "bugs" are `dataQuality != .detailedStages` filtering correctly.
47	- Distinguish `nil` vs `false` (tristate invariant #2). Never coerce.
48	- Concurrency: confirm `@MainActor`, `Task` boundaries, no strong service refs beyond DI.
49	- SwiftData: confirm migration stage, encryption transparency (`PersistenceJSON.decode`), file protection.
50	- HealthKit: read-only; no `toShare:` writes; no `healthkit` in `UIBackgroundModes`.
51	
52	### 6. Adding/upgrading features
53	- Reuse existing primitives: `BetterHealthCard`, `MetricGaugeView`, `SparklineView`, `circularMeanHour()`, `SleepSession.restorativeSleepDuration`. Never duplicate restorative/continuity math (invariant #11).
54	- New encrypted field? Update `PersistenceModels.swift` + bump schema + add migration stage.
55	- New CSV column? **Append only** — never reorder (invariant #9). Update both `nightly_research_rows.csv` and its metadata.
56	- New view model? `@Observable`, constructor-injected repos via `AppEnvironment`.
57	- Use design tokens (`BetterColors`, `BetterTypography`, `BetterSpacing`) and standard animations (spring 0.4/0.75, 0.45/0.82, easeInOut 0.5).
58	
59	### 7. Delegate intelligently
60	| Need | Delegate to |
61	|------|-------------|
62	| Open-ended search | Explore |
63	| Multi-step implementation plan | Plan |
64	| HealthKit / sleep math review | axiom-health |
65	| SwiftData / migrations review | axiom-data |
66	| Encryption / Keychain review | axiom-security |
67	| `@MainActor` / Task review | axiom-concurrency |
68	| View structure review | axiom-swiftui |
69	| Test plan / coverage | axiom-testing |
70	| Build/scheme/config | axiom-build |
71	| Performance | axiom-performance |
72	| Independent second opinion on a diff | ask user to run Codex; or run `code-review` skill before committing |
73	| UI behavior verification | `verify` / `run` skills — never claim a UI feature works without launching it |
74	
75	### 8. Build & test
76	Prefer XcodeBuildMCP when available. Fallback:
77	```
78	xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug
79	xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test
80	xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test -only BetterTests/<SuiteName>
81	xcodebuild -scheme BetterUITests test
82	```
83	
84	### 9. Commit hygiene
85	- Never commit unless explicitly asked.
86	- Never `--no-verify`.
87	- Never amend pushed commits.
88	- One commit per logical change.
89	- Commit message explains **why**, not what.
90	
91	### 10. Output style
92	Terse. No trailing summary restating the diff. `file:line` citations when referencing code. No emojis. Markdown headings only when the response has ≥3 distinct sections.
93	
94	---
95	
96	## The 11 Critical Invariants (cheat sheet)
97	
98	Always cross-reference these before proposing a change. Authoritative list lives in `CLAUDE.md` — this is a fast lookup.
99	
100	1. **Downward data flow.** Data → Repositories → Services → UI. Views never reach into repositories directly past the view model.
101	2. **Tristate booleans.** `Bool?` means {known true, known false, unknown}. Never coerce `nil → false`.
102	3. **HealthKit is read-only.** No `toShare:` writes. No `healthkit` in `UIBackgroundModes`.
103	4. **Encryption transparency.** All persisted JSON goes through `PersistenceJSON.encode/decode`. Files use complete file protection.
104	5. **Schema versioning.** Any `@Model` change → new `BetterSchemaVN` + `VersionedSchema` migration stage. No silent schema edits.
105	6. **Baseline integrity.** Baseline recomputes are explicit and idempotent. Any field that feeds a baseline must declare it.
106	7. **Protocol formula tracking.** Formula changes go through §7b of `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` — versioned, dated, with rationale.
107	8. **Data-quality gating.** Metrics that require `dataQuality == .detailedStages` must filter, not fabricate.
108	9. **CSV append-only.** `nightly_research_rows.csv` columns are append-only. Update CSV + metadata together.
109	10. **Concurrency boundaries.** UI on `@MainActor`. Services hop deliberately. No retained service refs outside DI.
110	11. **Single source of truth for sleep math.** `SleepSession.restorativeSleepDuration` and `circularMeanHour()` are canonical. Do not reimplement.
111	
112	---
113	
114	## Layer map
115	
116	```
117	Data          → @Model types, SwiftData store, PersistenceModels
118	Repositories  → LocalDataRepository, HealthKitRepository (protocols + impls + mocks)
119	Services      → Domain services, processors, encryption, baselines
120	UI            → @Observable view models → SwiftUI views (design tokens only)
121	```
122	Direction is strictly downward. Views never import `SwiftData` types directly; they consume view models.
123	
124	## File directory pointers
125	
126	- `Better/Models/` — `@Model` types, `PersistenceModels.swift`, schema versions (`BetterSchemaV1/V2/...`).
127	- `Better/Repositories/` — `LocalDataRepository`, `HealthKitRepository`, protocols, `MockLocalDataRepository`, `PreviewHealthKitRepository`.
128	- `Better/Processors/` — nightly processors, baseline computation, CSV row builders.
129	- `Better/Services/` — domain services, `AppEnvironment` (DI container).
130	- `Better/Security/` — `EncryptionService`, Keychain wrappers, `PersistenceJSON`.
131	- `Better/Features/` — feature folders. Each contains its `@Observable` view model + SwiftUI views.
132	- `Better/DesignSystem/` — `BetterColors`, `BetterTypography`, `BetterSpacing`, shared components (`BetterHealthCard`, `MetricGaugeView`, `SparklineView`).
133	- `BetterTests/` — unit + processor tests. `EncryptionServiceTests` requires `-parallel-testing-enabled NO`.
134	- `BetterUITests/` — UI tests.
135	- `CLAUDE.md`, `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` — invariants & architecture (read first).
136	- `nightly_research_rows.csv` + metadata — append-only research CSV.
137	
138	## Standard build/test commands
139	
140	```
141	xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug
142	xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test
143	xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test -only BetterTests/<SuiteName>
144	xcodebuild -scheme BetterUITests test
145	# EncryptionServiceTests:
146	xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test \
147	  -only BetterTests/EncryptionServiceTests \
148	  -parallel-testing-enabled NO
149	```
150	
151	---
152	
153	## Default response shape
154	
155	For any non-trivial task, your first reply should be the staged plan from §4 — not code. Wait for approval. Then implement step-by-step, citing `file:line` and noting which invariants each step touches.
156

---
name: better-ios-maintainer
description: Use when working on the Better iOS app (SwiftUI/SwiftData/HealthKit). Triggers for feature work, bug fixes, refactors, schema migrations, baseline/protocol/CSV changes, or any edit under Better/. Enforces the 11 critical invariants in CLAUDE.md and the architecture in APP_ARCHITECTURE.md.
---

# better-ios-maintainer

You are a senior iOS engineer who owns the Better codebase (SwiftUI · SwiftData · HealthKit · iOS 18+). You are not a generic assistant. You know this app's invariants, layering, and history. Behave accordingly.

## Operating environment

- Local Claude Code + Codex setup. You can delegate to subagents (Explore, Plan, general-purpose, code-review, axiom-* domain reviewers) and ask the user to invoke Codex for a second opinion on diffs.
- Repo root contains `CLAUDE.md` (invariants, conventions) and `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` (esp. §11 Metric Calculation Reference, §7b Protocol Formula Tracking). These are the source of truth — read them before answering, not after.

## Behavioral contract (non-negotiable)

### 1. Clarify before acting
Feature work with vague scope, missing acceptance criteria, or unclear UX → ask 1–3 sharp clarifying questions via AskUserQuestion before touching code. Never assume scope on features. Skip clarification only for obvious bug fixes with a clear repro.

### 2. Ground in the repo's docs first
Before proposing anything: read `CLAUDE.md` and the relevant section of `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` (§11, §7b for metrics/protocols). Cite `file:line` when referring to existing behavior. Treat "Critical Invariants" in CLAUDE.md as hard constraints — explicitly list which invariants the proposed change touches.

### 3. Map before changing
For any non-trivial task, spawn an **Explore** subagent to locate relevant files across the 4 layers. Identify:
- which `@Model` is affected
- which repository method
- which service
- which `@Observable` view model
- which view
Confirm data flow direction is downward only (Data → Repositories → Services → UI).

### 4. Staged plan, not a code dump
Output structure for any non-trivial change:
- **Understanding** — 1–2 sentences restating the ask.
- **Affected layers & files** — bulleted, with paths.
- **Invariants touched** — which of the 11 apply, and how the change respects them.
- **Migration impact** — needs `BetterSchemaV3`? VersionedSchema migration stage? Encryption-format change? Baseline recompute?
- **Plan** — numbered steps, each ≤1 file or 1 logical unit.
- **Test strategy** — `BetterTests` / `BetterUITests`, what to mock (`MockLocalDataRepository` / `PreviewHealthKitRepository`), whether `EncryptionServiceTests` needs `-parallel-testing-enabled NO`.
- **Risks & open questions.**

Implement only after the user approves the plan.

### 5. Debug like a senior iOS dev
- Reproduce mentally first: what HealthKit state, what SwiftData state, what user state triggers this?
- Check baseline/data-quality gating before assuming a calc bug — many "bugs" are `dataQuality != .detailedStages` filtering correctly.
- Distinguish `nil` vs `false` (tristate invariant #2). Never coerce.
- Concurrency: confirm `@MainActor`, `Task` boundaries, no strong service refs beyond DI.
- SwiftData: confirm migration stage, encryption transparency (`PersistenceJSON.decode`), file protection.
- HealthKit: read-only; no `toShare:` writes; no `healthkit` in `UIBackgroundModes`.

### 6. Adding/upgrading features
- Reuse existing primitives: `BetterHealthCard`, `MetricGaugeView`, `SparklineView`, `circularMeanHour()`, `SleepSession.restorativeSleepDuration`. Never duplicate restorative/continuity math (invariant #11).
- New encrypted field? Update `PersistenceModels.swift` + bump schema + add migration stage.
- New CSV column? **Append only** — never reorder (invariant #9). Update both `nightly_research_rows.csv` and its metadata.
- New view model? `@Observable`, constructor-injected repos via `AppEnvironment`.
- Use design tokens (`BetterColors`, `BetterTypography`, `BetterSpacing`) and standard animations (spring 0.4/0.75, 0.45/0.82, easeInOut 0.5).

### 7. Delegate intelligently
| Need | Delegate to |
|------|-------------|
| Open-ended search | Explore |
| Multi-step implementation plan | Plan |
| HealthKit / sleep math review | axiom-health |
| SwiftData / migrations review | axiom-data |
| Encryption / Keychain review | axiom-security |
| `@MainActor` / Task review | axiom-concurrency |
| View structure review | axiom-swiftui |
| Test plan / coverage | axiom-testing |
| Build/scheme/config | axiom-build |
| Performance | axiom-performance |
| Independent second opinion on a diff | ask user to run Codex; or run `code-review` skill before committing |
| UI behavior verification | `verify` / `run` skills — never claim a UI feature works without launching it |

### 8. Build & test
Prefer XcodeBuildMCP when available. Fallback:
```
xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug
xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test
xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test -only BetterTests/<SuiteName>
xcodebuild -scheme BetterUITests test
```

### 9. Commit hygiene
- Never commit unless explicitly asked.
- Never `--no-verify`.
- Never amend pushed commits.
- One commit per logical change.
- Commit message explains **why**, not what.

### 10. Output style
Terse. No trailing summary restating the diff. `file:line` citations when referencing code. No emojis. Markdown headings only when the response has ≥3 distinct sections.

---

## The 11 Critical Invariants (cheat sheet)

Always cross-reference these before proposing a change. Authoritative list lives in `CLAUDE.md` — this is a fast lookup.

1. **Downward data flow.** Data → Repositories → Services → UI. Views never reach into repositories directly past the view model.
2. **Tristate booleans.** `Bool?` means {known true, known false, unknown}. Never coerce `nil → false`.
3. **HealthKit is read-only.** No `toShare:` writes. No `healthkit` in `UIBackgroundModes`.
4. **Encryption transparency.** All persisted JSON goes through `PersistenceJSON.encode/decode`. Files use complete file protection.
5. **Schema versioning.** Any `@Model` change → new `BetterSchemaVN` + `VersionedSchema` migration stage. No silent schema edits.
6. **Baseline integrity.** Baseline recomputes are explicit and idempotent. Any field that feeds a baseline must declare it.
7. **Protocol formula tracking.** Formula changes go through §7b of `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` — versioned, dated, with rationale.
8. **Data-quality gating.** Metrics that require `dataQuality == .detailedStages` must filter, not fabricate.
9. **CSV append-only.** `nightly_research_rows.csv` columns are append-only. Update CSV + metadata together.
10. **Concurrency boundaries.** UI on `@MainActor`. Services hop deliberately. No retained service refs outside DI.
11. **Single source of truth for sleep math.** `SleepSession.restorativeSleepDuration` and `circularMeanHour()` are canonical. Do not reimplement.

---

## Layer map

```
Data          → @Model types, SwiftData store, PersistenceModels
Repositories  → LocalDataRepository, HealthKitRepository (protocols + impls + mocks)
Services      → Domain services, processors, encryption, baselines
UI            → @Observable view models → SwiftUI views (design tokens only)
```
Direction is strictly downward. Views never import `SwiftData` types directly; they consume view models.

## File directory pointers

- `Better/Models/` — `@Model` types, `PersistenceModels.swift`, schema versions (`BetterSchemaV1/V2/...`).
- `Better/Repositories/` — `LocalDataRepository`, `HealthKitRepository`, protocols, `MockLocalDataRepository`, `PreviewHealthKitRepository`.
- `Better/Processors/` — nightly processors, baseline computation, CSV row builders.
- `Better/Services/` — domain services, `AppEnvironment` (DI container).
- `Better/Security/` — `EncryptionService`, Keychain wrappers, `PersistenceJSON`.
- `Better/Features/` — feature folders. Each contains its `@Observable` view model + SwiftUI views.
- `Better/DesignSystem/` — `BetterColors`, `BetterTypography`, `BetterSpacing`, shared components (`BetterHealthCard`, `MetricGaugeView`, `SparklineView`).
- `BetterTests/` — unit + processor tests. `EncryptionServiceTests` requires `-parallel-testing-enabled NO`.
- `BetterUITests/` — UI tests.
- `CLAUDE.md`, `APP_ARCHITECTURE.md` — invariants & architecture (read first).
- `nightly_research_rows.csv` + metadata — append-only research CSV.

## Standard build/test commands

```
xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug
xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test
xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test -only BetterTests/<SuiteName>
xcodebuild -scheme BetterUITests test
# EncryptionServiceTests:
xcodebuild -scheme Better -configuration Debug test \
  -only BetterTests/EncryptionServiceTests \
  -parallel-testing-enabled NO
```

---

## Default response shape

For any non-trivial task, your first reply should be the staged plan from §4 — not code. Wait for approval. Then implement step-by-step, citing `file:line` and noting which invariants each step touches.

content.content:
1	# BetterSchemaV3 Implementation Manifest
2	
3	Goal: introduce `BetterSchemaV3` adding (A) `StoredInterventionWindow` model and (B) `versionID: UUID` field on `ProtocolBaselineSnapshot`. Read-only audit; nothing in this manifest has been edited.
4	
5	Absolute paths are rooted at `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/`.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Schema files
10	
11	All versioned schema declarations + the migration plan + container factory live in **one** file:
12	
13	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift`
14	  - `BetterSchemaV1: VersionedSchema` — declared at **L14–L34** (14 V1 models).
15	  - `BetterSchemaV2: VersionedSchema` — declared at **L36–L46** (V1 models + 4 Protocol Formula `@Model`s).
16	  - `BetterMigrationPlan: SchemaMigrationPlan` — declared at **L48–L55**. Currently a single `.lightweight` stage from V1 → V2 (L52–L54).
17	  - `BetterPersistenceContainerFactory` — **L57–L110**.
18	    - `currentSchema` at L58 (must be flipped to `BetterSchemaV3`).
19	    - `makeLiveContainer()` at L67 (uses `BetterMigrationPlan` — no change to wiring needed; just append a stage).
20	    - `makePreviewContainer()` at L78.
21	  - `PersistenceJSON.encode/decode` — L112–L136 (encrypted-JSON helper used by every blob field).
22	
23	No other file declares `VersionedSchema`, `SchemaMigrationPlan`, or registers models in a `ModelContainer` (verified via grep). All `@Model` types listed in `BetterSchemaV2.models` (L39–L44) are defined further down in this same file.
24	
25	---
26	
27	## 2. ProtocolFormulaVersion
28	
29	### Domain type
30	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Models/ProtocolFormulaModels.swift` — `struct ProtocolFormulaVersion` declared **L25–L84**.
31	  - Fields (L26–L41): `id: UUID`, `displayLabel: String`, `ordinalLabel: String` (computed at fetch time), `formulaText: String`, `components: [ProtocolFormulaComponent]`, `shippedOn: Date`, `colorHex: String`, `isActive: Bool`, `isImportedPlaceholder: Bool`, `archivedAt: Date?`, `createdAt: Date`, `updatedAt: Date`.
32	  - `defaultPaletteHexes` palette at L78–L83.
33	
34	### Persisted row
35	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift` — `@Model StoredProtocolFormulaVersion` **L798–L880**.
36	  - Stored cols (L800–L813): `id` (unique), `displayLabel`, `ordinalIndex`, `shippedOn`, `colorHex`, `isActive`, `isImportedPlaceholder`, `archivedAt`, `bodyData` (encrypted JSON of `ProtocolFormulaVersionBody { formulaText, components }` — L884–L888), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`.
37	  - `convenience init(domain:ordinalIndex:)` L841–L861, `toDomain(ordinalLabel:)` L863–L879.
38	
39	### `shippedOn` write sites (where it gets set)
40	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Models/ProtocolFormulaModels.swift:62` — initializer assigns `shippedOn`.
41	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolAdherenceMigrationService.swift:76` — placeholder migration uses derived legacy start date.
42	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:225` — `upsertVersion(for:shippedOn:)` user-driven seed.
43	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:241–L244` — `seedHistory` uses earliest painted date key.
44	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Onboarding/ProtocolOnboardingViewModel.swift:45` — `shippedOn: Date()` (note: comment at L37 says fabrication has been removed — but this fallback still exists).
45	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/FormulaSetup/ProtocolFormulaSetupViewModel.swift:52, :65` — setup flow assigns `Date()`.
46	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift:831` (init) and L852 (convenience), L871 (toDomain).
47	
48	### `shippedOn` read / ordering sites (every site)
49	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:539` — `SortDescriptor(\.shippedOn)` when computing `ordinalIndex` on save.
50	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:542` — filter `$0.shippedOn <= version.shippedOn`.
51	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:591` — primary sort for `fetchAllFormulaVersions`.
52	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:92` — sort `< shippedOn`.
53	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:303` — best-version tiebreak compares `shippedOn`.
54	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:175` — derives baseline cutoff: `versions.compactMap({ Self.sleepDateKey(for: $0.shippedOn) }).min()`.
55	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:362` — comment: helper that converts `shippedOn` → date key.
56	
57	---
58	
59	## 3. ProtocolBaselineSnapshot
60	
61	### Domain type
62	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Models/ProtocolFormulaModels.swift` — `struct ProtocolBaselineSnapshot` **L181–L286**.
63	  - Fields (L182–L210): `id, frozenAt, windowStart, windowEnd, validNightCount`, restorative-sleep means/stds, `continuityCategoryDistribution`, `isInsufficient`, P0-4 extended means/stds (`meanDeepMin … stdSleepScore`).
64	  - **No** `versionID` field today. (B) adds it here.
65	
66	### Persisted row
67	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift` — `@Model StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` **L1000–L1092**.
68	  - Stored cols (L1002–L1009): `id` (unique), `frozenAt`, `windowStart`, `windowEnd`, `validNightCount`, `isInsufficient`, `bodyData` (encrypted JSON `ProtocolBaselineSnapshotBody`, L1094–L1114).
69	  - `init(domain:)` L1029, `toDomain()` L1062.
70	
71	### Construction sites (`ProtocolBaselineSnapshot(...)` calls)
72	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolBaselineService.swift:59–L84` — `freezeBaseline` builds the snapshot (the only production constructor).
73	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolBaselineService.swift:111+` — `augmentBaselineWithExtendedMetricsIfNeeded` *mutates* an existing snapshot via `var augmented = existing` (L111) and re-saves.
74	- Tests:
75	  - `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/BetterTests/LocalDataRepositoryTests.swift:820–L848` — `static func protocolBaselineSnapshot()` factory.
76	  - `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/BetterTests/ProtocolFormulaTrackingTests.swift:236–L237` (`makeBaseline()` helper).
77	  - `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/BetterTests/ProtocolFormulaTrackingTests.swift:540–L554` — explicit construction in augmentation test.
78	  - `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/BetterTests/ProtocolFormulaCatalogServiceTests.swift:30` — best-version test fixture.
79	
80	### Read sites
81	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:697` — `fetchBaselineSnapshot()` (latest by `frozenAt` desc, `fetchLimit = 1`).
82	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolBaselineService.swift:39, :97` — internal reads in freeze + augment.
83	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaAnalysisService.swift:125` — used inside `impactSummary`.
84	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaInsightsService.swift:15` — used inside `insights(for:)`.
85	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:168, :177` — Home refresh.
86	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/AllMetrics/ProtocolAllMetricsViewModel.swift:39` — All Metrics reload.
87	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/VersionDive/ProtocolVersionDiveViewModel.swift:36` — Version Dive reload.
88	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Timeline/ProtocolTimelineViewModel.swift:58` — Timeline reload.
89	
90	### Singleton / cardinality
91	- **YES, currently a singleton** (one row).
92	  - `LocalDataRepository.saveBaselineSnapshot` (`/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:685–L695`) explicitly deletes every existing row before inserting. Inline comment at L689: *"Only one baseline row is ever persisted — replace any existing."*
93	  - `fetchBaselineSnapshot` (L697–L703) sorts by `frozenAt` desc and applies `fetchLimit = 1`.
94	  - Repository protocol method is parameterless: `func fetchBaselineSnapshot() async throws -> ProtocolBaselineSnapshot?` (`RepositoryProtocols.swift:95`).

186	## 7. Tests
187	
188	Mock used: `MockLocalDataRepository` lives at `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/MockLocalDataRepository.swift` (under `Better/`, not under `BetterTests/`). It does **not** implement Protocol Formula methods; it relies on the protocol-extension no-op defaults at `RepositoryProtocols.swift:216–L229`. Only Sleep / activity / sleep-mode / context-entry methods are exercised.

190	Tests dedicated to Protocol Formula use a private actor `ProtocolFormulaMemoryRepo` defined inline at `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/BetterTests/ProtocolFormulaTrackingTests.swift:17–L213`. This actor has real Protocol Formula state and full ordering semantics. The new V3 fields will need to be added here (see (8)).

192	### Existing tests — methods that touch versions or baseline snapshots
193	
194	`/private/tmp/better-schemav3/BetterTests/ProtocolFormulaTrackingTests.swift`:
195	- `formulaVersions` store at L26, `baseline` at L29.

213	
214	## 8. Migration plan sketch (BetterSchemaV3)
215	
216	Update `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift`:
217	
218	1. Add new model `StoredInterventionWindow` (mirrors domain `StoredInterventionWindow { id: UUID, versionID: UUID, startedAt: Date, endedAt: Date?, phase: Phase }`). `phase` likely stored as `phaseRawValue: String` for SwiftData compatibility (consistent with the rest of this file's pattern; see e.g. `StoredAlert.kindRawValue` at L550).
219	2. Add `versionID: UUID` field to `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` (new column — V2's row had no version association). Update `init(domain:)` (L1029) and `toDomain()` (L1062) to round-trip it. Update domain `ProtocolBaselineSnapshot` in `ProtocolFormulaModels.swift:181–L286` accordingly.
220	3. Append `BetterSchemaV3: VersionedSchema` containing V2 models + the new `StoredInterventionWindow` and the *modified* `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot`. The modified snapshot type forces the stage to be **custom**, not `.lightweight`.
221	4. Append a `MigrationStage.custom(fromVersion: BetterSchemaV2.self, toVersion: BetterSchemaV3.self, willMigrate:didMigrate:)` to `BetterMigrationPlan.stages` (L52).
222	5. Flip `BetterPersistenceContainerFactory.currentSchema` (L58) to `BetterSchemaV3`.
223	
224	### `willMigrate` / `didMigrate` operations (plain English)
225	
226	`willMigrate`:
227	- *(no-op recommended)*. SwiftData's custom-migration semantics let us do all transformation work in `didMigrate` once both schemas are in scope. We don't need to read pre-migration encrypted blobs because the snapshot's `bodyData` shape is unchanged — only a new top-level column is added.
228	
229	`didMigrate(context:)`:
230	1. **(A) Synthesize one `StoredInterventionWindow` per existing version.**
231	   - Fetch all V2 versions sorted by `(shippedOn ASC, createdAt ASC)` (matches the existing read order in `LocalDataRepository.fetchAllFormulaVersions:591`).
232	   - For each version `v[i]` with the sorted list `v[0..n)`:
233	     - Insert `StoredInterventionWindow(id: UUID(), versionID: v[i].id, startedAt: v[i].shippedOn, endedAt: i+1 < n ? v[i+1].shippedOn : nil, phase: .intervention)`.
234	   - Note: the spec says "one .intervention window per existing ProtocolFormulaVersion" — archived/imported-placeholder versions are still iterated (they have `shippedOn`); confirm with product whether `archivedAt != nil` should be skipped or get a closed window with `endedAt = archivedAt`. Current spec language: every existing version gets one window — implement literally.
235	
236	2. **(B) Backfill `versionID` on the singleton `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot`.**
237	   - Fetch all baseline snapshot rows (cardinality is ≤ 1 in practice — see §3 singleton invariant — but iterate defensively).
238	   - Compute oldest version: same sort as above, take `first`.
239	   - If oldest exists, set `snapshot.versionID = oldest.id` for each existing snapshot row; otherwise the snapshot is orphaned (very rare — possible only if a baseline exists with zero versions, which the freeze flow does not produce). Recommended fallback: leave the row but assign `UUID()` and log; or delete orphans. Choose deletion for simplicity (the freeze flow rebuilds snapshots).
240	   - `try context.save()`.
241	
242	### Encrypted-JSON column shape changes
243	
244	- `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot.bodyData` (`PersistenceModels.swift:1009`, body struct `ProtocolBaselineSnapshotBody` L1094–L1114): **no change**. The new `versionID` is added as a SwiftData column, NOT into the JSON body. This avoids touching `PersistenceJSON.encode/decode` paths and preserves the legacy-decode fallback test at `LocalDataRepositoryTests.swift:183`.
245	- `StoredProtocolFormulaVersion.bodyData` (L811, body struct L884–L888): no change.
246	- `StoredProtocolNightLog.bodyData` (L901): no change.
247	- `StoredInterventionWindow`: phase is stored as a raw-valued string column; no JSON blob needed (small fixed schema).
248	
249	If a future PR moves any of these fields *out of* the JSON blob, that is a separate, additive concern — not required for V3.
250	
251	### Code surface that must change (besides PersistenceModels.swift)
252	
253	- `RepositoryProtocols.swift:94–L95` — likely add `fetchBaselineSnapshot(versionID:)` and keep the singleton method as a convenience that returns "current version's snapshot". Update default no-op extension at L228–L229.
254	- `LocalDataRepository.swift:685–L703` — `saveBaselineSnapshot` must NOT delete all rows (since baselines are now per-version). Replace the "delete all then insert" with upsert keyed by `versionID`. `fetchBaselineSnapshot` needs a version-keyed variant.
255	- `ProtocolBaselineService.swift:38, :85, :96, :124` — freeze is called per version transition; existing-snapshot reuse check at L39 must compare `versionID` not just existence.
256	- `ProtocolFormulaAnalysisService.impactSummary` (L125) — load the per-version baseline.
257	- `ProtocolFormulaInsightsService.swift:15` — same.
258	- `ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel:168/L177`, `ProtocolVersionDiveViewModel:36`, `ProtocolAllMetricsViewModel:39`, `ProtocolTimelineViewModel:58` — switch to per-version fetch.
259	- New service or extension to register a `StoredInterventionWindow` whenever a new `ProtocolFormulaVersion` is saved (write-time), to keep windows in sync after migration.
260	- `LocalDataRepository.deleteAllHealthData` (L749+) and `migrateToEncryptedStorage` (L820+) and `fetchDataInventory` (L876+) — add the new `StoredInterventionWindow` table.
261	- Tests in §7 — add `versionID` to `makeBaseline`/`protocolBaselineSnapshot()` factories and `LegacyProtocolBaselineSnapshotBody` decode test (which still must succeed: legacy rows have no `versionID` column post-migration unless migration ran — guarantee via a test that boots a V2 store, runs migration, and asserts `versionID == oldest.id`).
262	
263	---
264	
265	## 9. Risk flags
266	
267	### Code that assumes a singleton baseline (`.first`, `fetchLimit = 1`, count==1)
268	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:685–L695` — `saveBaselineSnapshot` deletes every existing row then inserts. **Will silently wipe other versions' baselines after V3 if not changed.** Highest-risk site.
269	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:701` — `descriptor.fetchLimit = 1` in `fetchBaselineSnapshot`.
270	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/RepositoryProtocols.swift:95` — protocol method has no `versionID` parameter.
271	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolBaselineService.swift:39` — `if !force, let existing = …` short-circuits on *any* baseline; will skip per-version freeze on V3.
272	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolBaselineService.swift:97` — `augmentBaselineWithExtendedMetricsIfNeeded` mutates the global singleton; needs to iterate per version.
273	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaAnalysisService.swift:125` — `impactSummary` consumes one global baseline.
274	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaInsightsService.swift:15` — single baseline used for *every* version's insight.
275	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:270` — `bestVersion(versions:rollups:baseline:)` takes one baseline; needs a baseline-by-versionID map.
276	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:168, :177, :211` — single baseline value passed into `bestVersion`.
277	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/AllMetrics/ProtocolAllMetricsViewModel.swift:39, :47, :70` — single baseline value drives every chart line.
278	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/VersionDive/ProtocolVersionDiveViewModel.swift:36, :59, :63, :73` — single baseline used for comparison bars.
279	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Timeline/ProtocolTimelineViewModel.swift:58, :92, :112` — single baseline gates "best lift" tile.
280	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/RepositoryProtocols.swift:134, :154, :173` — `LocalDataInventory.protocolBaselineSnapshotCount` exposes a count that consumers may have implicitly assumed is 0/1.
281	
282	### Code that reads `ProtocolFormulaVersion.shippedOn` ordering — could break with windows
283	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:539, :542` — ordinal index derives from `shippedOn` ordering; identical `shippedOn` collisions are tolerated only because `<= shippedOn` count is used. With windows, identical timestamps risk overlapping windows.
284	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:591` — primary sort uses `shippedOn` then `createdAt`; the migration must use this **same secondary key** when generating `endedAt` so two versions shipped on the same day still produce non-overlapping windows.
285	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:92, :303` — sort/tiebreak on `shippedOn` (must reconcile with window-derived ordering once windows exist).
286	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:175` — earliest `shippedOn` is treated as the *protocol-tracking start* for baseline cutoff. With explicit intervention windows, this should derive from the oldest window's `startedAt` instead.
287	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolAdherenceMigrationService.swift:76, :110–L118` — legacy migration path also infers a "start date" that, post-V3, should be the placeholder version's *intervention window* start.
288	- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Onboarding/ProtocolOnboardingViewModel.swift:45` — onboarding default `shippedOn: Date()` will create a V3 window with `endedAt = nil` if a previous version exists with a *later* `shippedOn` — the migration logic must handle "incoming new version with `shippedOn < latest`" (closes the previous window retroactively, which `saveFormulaVersion` does not currently do).
289	
290	### Other notable risks
291	- The legacy-decode test (`LocalDataRepositoryTests.swift:183`) directly constructs a V2 `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` using its current init signature. After (B), this fixture must include `versionID:` (or the test must use a SQLite store snapshotted at V2 + run migration).
292	- `ProtocolBaselineSnapshot` is `Codable` (JSON-encoded into `bodyData` after V3 changes too). Adding `versionID` only to the SwiftData column (and not to the body struct) keeps wire compatibility and avoids the `PersistenceJSON.decode` fallback path needing a default. This is the recommended path.
293	- `ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.upsertVersion` (L196) does not currently emit an intervention window. Post-V3, version save and window creation must be transactional or there will be drift.
294	- The V2 schema declaration explicitly forbids mutation: header comment at `PersistenceModels.swift:11–L12` — *"Never mutate `BetterSchemaV1` / `BetterSchemaV2` after they ship."* The new column on `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` therefore must live on the V3 copy of the type. This requires SwiftData's ability to reference both versions of the same `@Model` class — which in practice means defining the model once and using migration stages to bridge. Confirm via Apple's `VersionedSchema` docs whether the V2 declaration still type-checks against the *same* class reference; if not, the V3 work needs separate `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshotV2` / `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshotV3` shadow types — a meaningful scope expansion.
295	
296

# BetterSchemaV3 Implementation Manifest

Goal: introduce `BetterSchemaV3` adding (A) `StoredInterventionWindow` model and (B) `versionID: UUID` field on `ProtocolBaselineSnapshot`. Read-only audit; nothing in this manifest has been edited.

Absolute paths are rooted at `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/`.

---

## 1. Schema files

All versioned schema declarations + the migration plan + container factory live in **one** file:

- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift`
  - `BetterSchemaV1: VersionedSchema` — declared at **L14–L34** (14 V1 models).
  - `BetterSchemaV2: VersionedSchema` — declared at **L36–L46** (V1 models + 4 Protocol Formula `@Model`s).
  - `BetterMigrationPlan: SchemaMigrationPlan` — declared at **L48–L55**. Currently a single `.lightweight` stage from V1 → V2 (L52–L54).
  - `BetterPersistenceContainerFactory` — **L57–L110**.
    - `currentSchema` at L58 (must be flipped to `BetterSchemaV3`).
    - `makeLiveContainer()` at L67 (uses `BetterMigrationPlan` — no change to wiring needed; just append a stage).
    - `makePreviewContainer()` at L78.
  - `PersistenceJSON.encode/decode` — L112–L136 (encrypted-JSON helper used by every blob field).

No other file declares `VersionedSchema`, `SchemaMigrationPlan`, or registers models in a `ModelContainer` (verified via grep). All `@Model` types listed in `BetterSchemaV2.models` (L39–L44) are defined further down in this same file.

---

## 2. ProtocolFormulaVersion

### Domain type
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Models/ProtocolFormulaModels.swift` — `struct ProtocolFormulaVersion` declared **L25–L84**.
  - Fields (L26–L41): `id: UUID`, `displayLabel: String`, `ordinalLabel: String` (computed at fetch time), `formulaText: String`, `components: [ProtocolFormulaComponent]`, `shippedOn: Date`, `colorHex: String`, `isActive: Bool`, `isImportedPlaceholder: Bool`, `archivedAt: Date?`, `createdAt: Date`, `updatedAt: Date`.
  - `defaultPaletteHexes` palette at L78–L83.

### Persisted row
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift` — `@Model StoredProtocolFormulaVersion` **L798–L880**.
  - Stored cols (L800–L813): `id` (unique), `displayLabel`, `ordinalIndex`, `shippedOn`, `colorHex`, `isActive`, `isImportedPlaceholder`, `archivedAt`, `bodyData` (encrypted JSON of `ProtocolFormulaVersionBody { formulaText, components }` — L884–L888), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`.
  - `convenience init(domain:ordinalIndex:)` L841–L861, `toDomain(ordinalLabel:)` L863–L879.

### `shippedOn` write sites (where it gets set)
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Models/ProtocolFormulaModels.swift:62` — initializer assigns `shippedOn`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolAdherenceMigrationService.swift:76` — placeholder migration uses derived legacy start date.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:225` — `upsertVersion(for:shippedOn:)` user-driven seed.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:241–L244` — `seedHistory` uses earliest painted date key.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Onboarding/ProtocolOnboardingViewModel.swift:45` — `shippedOn: Date()` (note: comment at L37 says fabrication has been removed — but this fallback still exists).
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/FormulaSetup/ProtocolFormulaSetupViewModel.swift:52, :65` — setup flow assigns `Date()`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift:831` (init) and L852 (convenience), L871 (toDomain).

### `shippedOn` read / ordering sites (every site)
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:539` — `SortDescriptor(\.shippedOn)` when computing `ordinalIndex` on save.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:542` — filter `$0.shippedOn <= version.shippedOn`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:591` — primary sort for `fetchAllFormulaVersions`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:92` — sort `< shippedOn`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:303` — best-version tiebreak compares `shippedOn`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:175` — derives baseline cutoff: `versions.compactMap({ Self.sleepDateKey(for: $0.shippedOn) }).min()`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:362` — comment: helper that converts `shippedOn` → date key.

---

## 3. ProtocolBaselineSnapshot

### Domain type
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Models/ProtocolFormulaModels.swift` — `struct ProtocolBaselineSnapshot` **L181–L286**.
  - Fields (L182–L210): `id, frozenAt, windowStart, windowEnd, validNightCount`, restorative-sleep means/stds, `continuityCategoryDistribution`, `isInsufficient`, P0-4 extended means/stds (`meanDeepMin … stdSleepScore`).
  - **No** `versionID` field today. (B) adds it here.

### Persisted row
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift` — `@Model StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` **L1000–L1092**.
  - Stored cols (L1002–L1009): `id` (unique), `frozenAt`, `windowStart`, `windowEnd`, `validNightCount`, `isInsufficient`, `bodyData` (encrypted JSON `ProtocolBaselineSnapshotBody`, L1094–L1114).

## 8. Migration plan sketch (BetterSchemaV3)

Update `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Persistence/PersistenceModels.swift`:

1. Add new model `StoredInterventionWindow` (mirrors domain `StoredInterventionWindow { id: UUID, versionID: UUID, startedAt: Date, endedAt: Date?, phase: Phase }`). `phase` likely stored as `phaseRawValue: String` for SwiftData compatibility (consistent with the rest of this file's pattern; see e.g. `StoredAlert.kindRawValue` at L550).
2. Add `versionID: UUID` field to `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` (new column — V2's row had no version association). Update `init(domain:)` (L1029) and `toDomain()` (L1062) to round-trip it. Update domain `ProtocolBaselineSnapshot` in `ProtocolFormulaModels.swift:181–L286` accordingly.
3. Append `BetterSchemaV3: VersionedSchema` containing V2 models + the new `StoredInterventionWindow` and the *modified* `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot`. The modified snapshot type forces the stage to be **custom**, not `.lightweight`.
4. Append a `MigrationStage.custom(fromVersion: BetterSchemaV2.self, toVersion: BetterSchemaV3.self, willMigrate:didMigrate:)` to `BetterMigrationPlan.stages` (L52).
5. Flip `BetterPersistenceContainerFactory.currentSchema` (L58) to `BetterSchemaV3`.

### `willMigrate` / `didMigrate` operations (plain English)

`willMigrate`:
- *(no-op recommended)*. SwiftData's custom-migration semantics let us do all transformation work in `didMigrate` once both schemas are in scope. We don't need to read pre-migration encrypted blobs because the snapshot's `bodyData` shape is unchanged — only a new top-level column is added.

`didMigrate(context:)`:
1. **(A) Synthesize one `StoredInterventionWindow` per existing version.**
   - Fetch all V2 versions sorted by `(shippedOn ASC, createdAt ASC)` (matches the existing read order in `LocalDataRepository.fetchAllFormulaVersions:591`).
   - For each version `v[i]` with the sorted list `v[0..n)`:
     - Insert `StoredInterventionWindow(id: UUID(), versionID: v[i].id, startedAt: v[i].shippedOn, endedAt: i+1 < n ? v[i+1].shippedOn : nil, phase: .intervention)`.
   - Note: the spec says "one .intervention window per existing ProtocolFormulaVersion" — archived/imported-placeholder versions are still iterated (they have `shippedOn`); confirm with product whether `archivedAt != nil` should be skipped or get a closed window with `endedAt = archivedAt`. Current spec language: every existing version gets one window — implement literally.

2. **(B) Backfill `versionID` on the singleton `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot`.**
   - Fetch all baseline snapshot rows (cardinality is ≤ 1 in practice — see §3 singleton invariant — but iterate defensively).
   - Compute oldest version: same sort as above, take `first`.
   - If oldest exists, set `snapshot.versionID = oldest.id` for each existing snapshot row; otherwise the snapshot is orphaned (very rare — possible only if a baseline exists with zero versions, which the freeze flow does not produce). Recommended fallback: leave the row but assign `UUID()` and log; or delete orphans. Choose deletion for simplicity (the freeze flow rebuilds snapshots).
   - `try context.save()`.

### Encrypted-JSON column shape changes

- `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot.bodyData` (`PersistenceModels.swift:1009`, body struct `ProtocolBaselineSnapshotBody` L1094–L1114): **no change**. The new `versionID` is added as a SwiftData column, NOT into the JSON body. This avoids touching `PersistenceJSON.encode/decode` paths and preserves the legacy-decode fallback test at `LocalDataRepositoryTests.swift:183`.
- `StoredProtocolFormulaVersion.bodyData` (L811, body struct L884–L888): no change.
- `StoredProtocolNightLog.bodyData` (L901): no change.
- `StoredInterventionWindow`: phase is stored as a raw-valued string column; no JSON blob needed (small fixed schema).

If a future PR moves any of these fields *out of* the JSON blob, that is a separate, additive concern — not required for V3.

### Code surface that must change (besides PersistenceModels.swift)

- `RepositoryProtocols.swift:94–L95` — likely add `fetchBaselineSnapshot(versionID:)` and keep the singleton method as a convenience that returns "current version's snapshot". Update default no-op extension at L228–L229.
- `LocalDataRepository.swift:685–L703` — `saveBaselineSnapshot` must NOT delete all rows (since baselines are now per-version). Replace the "delete all then insert" with upsert keyed by `versionID`. `fetchBaselineSnapshot` needs a version-keyed variant.
- `ProtocolBaselineService.swift:38, :85, :96, :124` — freeze is called per version transition; existing-snapshot reuse check at L39 must compare `versionID` not just existence.
- `ProtocolFormulaAnalysisService.impactSummary` (L125) — load the per-version baseline.
- `ProtocolFormulaInsightsService.swift:15` — same.
- `ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel:168/L177`, `ProtocolVersionDiveViewModel:36`, `ProtocolAllMetricsViewModel:39`, `ProtocolTimelineViewModel:58` — switch to per-version fetch.
- New service or extension to register a `StoredInterventionWindow` whenever a new `ProtocolFormulaVersion` is saved (write-time), to keep windows in sync after migration.
- `LocalDataRepository.deleteAllHealthData` (L749+) and `migrateToEncryptedStorage` (L820+) and `fetchDataInventory` (L876+) — add the new `StoredInterventionWindow` table.
- Tests in §7 — add `versionID` to `makeBaseline`/`protocolBaselineSnapshot()` factories and `LegacyProtocolBaselineSnapshotBody` decode test (which still must succeed: legacy rows have no `versionID` column post-migration unless migration ran — guarantee via a test that boots a V2 store, runs migration, and asserts `versionID == oldest.id`).

## 9. Risk flags

### Code that assumes a singleton baseline (`.first`, `fetchLimit = 1`, count==1)
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:685–L695` — `saveBaselineSnapshot` deletes every existing row then inserts. **Will silently wipe other versions' baselines after V3 if not changed.** Highest-risk site.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:701` — `descriptor.fetchLimit = 1` in `fetchBaselineSnapshot`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/RepositoryProtocols.swift:95` — protocol method has no `versionID` parameter.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolBaselineService.swift:39` — `if !force, let existing = …` short-circuits on *any* baseline; will skip per-version freeze on V3.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolBaselineService.swift:97` — `augmentBaselineWithExtendedMetricsIfNeeded` mutates the global singleton; needs to iterate per version.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaAnalysisService.swift:125` — `impactSummary` consumes one global baseline.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaInsightsService.swift:15` — single baseline used for *every* version's insight.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:270` — `bestVersion(versions:rollups:baseline:)` takes one baseline; needs a baseline-by-versionID map.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:168, :177, :211` — single baseline value passed into `bestVersion`.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/AllMetrics/ProtocolAllMetricsViewModel.swift:39, :47, :70` — single baseline value drives every chart line.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/VersionDive/ProtocolVersionDiveViewModel.swift:36, :59, :63, :73` — single baseline used for comparison bars.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Timeline/ProtocolTimelineViewModel.swift:58, :92, :112` — single baseline gates "best lift" tile.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/RepositoryProtocols.swift:134, :154, :173` — `LocalDataInventory.protocolBaselineSnapshotCount` exposes a count that consumers may have implicitly assumed is 0/1.

### Code that reads `ProtocolFormulaVersion.shippedOn` ordering — could break with windows
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:539, :542` — ordinal index derives from `shippedOn` ordering; identical `shippedOn` collisions are tolerated only because `<= shippedOn` count is used. With windows, identical timestamps risk overlapping windows.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Repositories/LocalDataRepository.swift:591` — primary sort uses `shippedOn` then `createdAt`; the migration must use this **same secondary key** when generating `endedAt` so two versions shipped on the same day still produce non-overlapping windows.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.swift:92, :303` — sort/tiebreak on `shippedOn` (must reconcile with window-derived ordering once windows exist).
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Home/ProtocolFormulaHomeViewModel.swift:175` — earliest `shippedOn` is treated as the *protocol-tracking start* for baseline cutoff. With explicit intervention windows, this should derive from the oldest window's `startedAt` instead.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Core/Services/ProtocolAdherenceMigrationService.swift:76, :110–L118` — legacy migration path also infers a "start date" that, post-V3, should be the placeholder version's *intervention window* start.
- `/private/tmp/better-schemav3/Better/Features/ProtocolFormula/Onboarding/ProtocolOnboardingViewModel.swift:45` — onboarding default `shippedOn: Date()` will create a V3 window with `endedAt = nil` if a previous version exists with a *later* `shippedOn` — the migration logic must handle "incoming new version with `shippedOn < latest`" (closes the previous window retroactively, which `saveFormulaVersion` does not currently do).

### Other notable risks
- The legacy-decode test (`LocalDataRepositoryTests.swift:183`) directly constructs a V2 `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` using its current init signature. After (B), this fixture must include `versionID:` (or the test must use a SQLite store snapshotted at V2 + run migration).
- `ProtocolBaselineSnapshot` is `Codable` (JSON-encoded into `bodyData` after V3 changes too). Adding `versionID` only to the SwiftData column (and not to the body struct) keeps wire compatibility and avoids the `PersistenceJSON.decode` fallback path needing a default. This is the recommended path.
- `ProtocolFormulaCatalogService.upsertVersion` (L196) does not currently emit an intervention window. Post-V3, version save and window creation must be transactional or there will be drift.
- The V2 schema declaration explicitly forbids mutation: header comment at `PersistenceModels.swift:11–L12` — *"Never mutate `BetterSchemaV1` / `BetterSchemaV2` after they ship."* The new column on `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshot` therefore must live on the V3 copy of the type. This requires SwiftData's ability to reference both versions of the same `@Model` class — which in practice means defining the model once and using migration stages to bridge. Confirm via Apple's `VersionedSchema` docs whether the V2 declaration still type-checks against the *same* class reference; if not, the V3 work needs separate `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshotV2` / `StoredProtocolBaselineSnapshotV3` shadow types — a meaningful scope expansion.

75	nonisolated enum BetterPersistenceContainerFactory {
76	    private static var currentSchema: Schema { Schema(versionedSchema: BetterSchemaV3.self) }
77	
78	    /// Builds the live SwiftData container with `BetterMigrationPlan` wired in.
79	    ///
80	    /// Unlike the pre-V2 implementation, this no longer silently wipes the store on init
81	    /// failure. User-entered Protocol Formula data isn't recoverable from HealthKit, so a
82	    /// silent reset would destroy work. On migration failure the error propagates and the
83	    /// app surfaces a non-destructive blocking recovery state on next launch (the user
84	    /// must opt into "Reset all local data" from Settings).
85	    static func makeLiveContainer() throws -> ModelContainer {
86	        let config = ModelConfiguration(isStoredInMemoryOnly: false)
87	        let container = try ModelContainer(
88	            for: currentSchema,
89	            migrationPlan: BetterMigrationPlan.self,
90	            configurations: config
91	        )
92	        applyFileProtection(to: container.configurations.first?.url)
93	        return container
94	    }
95	
96	    static func makePreviewContainer() throws -> ModelContainer {
97	        try ModelContainer(
98	            for: currentSchema,
99	            configurations: ModelConfiguration(isStoredInMemoryOnly: true)
100	        )
101	    }
102	
103	    /// Upgrades SQLite store files to FileProtectionType.completeUnlessOpen.
104	    ///
105	    /// We intentionally use completeUnlessOpen (not .complete) because background tasks
106	    /// (BGAppRefreshTask) fire while the device is locked. With .complete the SQLite
107	    /// WAL files become inaccessible mid-write, corrupting them and crashing the next
108	    /// foreground open. completeUnlessOpen keeps already-open files accessible across
109	    /// a lock event while still protecting files that are closed at rest.
110	    private static func applyFileProtection(to storeURL: URL?) {
111	        guard let storeURL else { return }
112	        let fileManager = FileManager.default
113	        // SQLite stores consist of up to three files.
114	        let candidatePaths = [
115	            storeURL.path,
116	            storeURL.path + "-shm",
117	            storeURL.path + "-wal"
118	        ]
119	        for path in candidatePaths {
120	            guard fileManager.fileExists(atPath: path) else { continue }
121	            try? fileManager.setAttributes(
122	                [.protectionKey: FileProtectionType.completeUnlessOpen],
123	                ofItemAtPath: path
124	            )
125	        }
126	    }
127	
128	}
129	
130	enum PersistenceJSON {
131	    /// Encodes value to JSON then encrypts the bytes with AES-256-GCM.
132	    /// If encryption is unavailable (e.g. Keychain locked), the plain JSON is
133	    /// returned so writes never fail silently — the store's own file protection
134	    /// still applies.
135	    nonisolated static func encode<T: Encodable>(_ value: T) throws -> Data {
136	        let encoder = JSONEncoder()
137	        encoder.dateEncodingStrategy = .iso8601
138	        let jsonData = try encoder.encode(value)
139	        return try EncryptionService.shared.encrypt(jsonData)
140	    }
141	
142	    /// Decrypts the data and decodes it.  If decryption fails the data is
143	    /// treated as plain JSON — this provides transparent migration from the
144	    /// pre-encryption storage format without a schema change.
145	    nonisolated static func decode<T: Decodable>(_ type: T.Type, from data: Data) throws -> T {
146	        let decoder = JSONDecoder()
147	        decoder.dateDecodingStrategy = .iso8601
148	        if let decrypted = try? EncryptionService.shared.decrypt(data),
149	           let value = try? decoder.decode(type, from: decrypted) {
150	            return value
151	        }
152	        return try decoder.decode(type, from: data)
153	    }
154	}

177	nonisolated enum ProtocolFormulaRepositoryError: Error, Equatable, Sendable {
178	    /// Raised when a save would edit `formulaText` or `components` of a version that
179	    /// already has at least one ProtocolNightLog referencing it (and is not an
180	    /// imported-placeholder still awaiting backfill).
181	    case formulaTextLocked(versionID: UUID)
182	    /// Raised by `saveBaselineSnapshot` when the snapshot would be persisted with
183	    /// zero valid nights — explicitly disallowed.
184	    case baselineSnapshotEmpty
185	}

187	// MARK: - HealthKit fallback states
188	
189	/// Richer data-quality states shown in the Sleep tab, distinct from
190	/// HealthAuthorizationPresentationState (which covers permissions only).
191	enum HealthKitFallbackState: Sendable, Equatable {
192	    case permissionDenied
193	    case baselineBuilding(nightsLogged: Int, nightsNeeded: Int)
194	    case noSleepStages

25	nonisolated struct ProtocolFormulaVersion: Codable, Hashable, Sendable, Identifiable {
26	    var id: UUID
27	    /// User-editable label. Empty string means "fall back to the derived ordinal label".
28	    var displayLabel: String
29	    /// Derived presentation fallback ("V1", "V2", …) computed at fetch time from
30	    /// `shippedOn` ordering. Stored here only so equality checks stay total — the
31	    /// authoritative ordinal value is set by the repository on read.
32	    var ordinalLabel: String
33	    var formulaText: String
34	    var components: [ProtocolFormulaComponent]
35	    var shippedOn: Date
36	    var colorHex: String
37	    var isActive: Bool
38	    var isImportedPlaceholder: Bool
39	    var archivedAt: Date?
40	    var createdAt: Date
41	    var updatedAt: Date
42	
43	    init(
44	        id: UUID = UUID(),
45	        displayLabel: String = "",
46	        ordinalLabel: String = "",
47	        formulaText: String = "",
48	        components: [ProtocolFormulaComponent] = [],
49	        shippedOn: Date = Date(),
50	        colorHex: String = ProtocolFormulaVersion.defaultPaletteHexes[0],
51	        isActive: Bool = true,
52	        isImportedPlaceholder: Bool = false,
53	        archivedAt: Date? = nil,
54	        createdAt: Date = Date(),
55	        updatedAt: Date = Date()
56	    ) {
57	        self.id = id
58	        self.displayLabel = displayLabel
59	        self.ordinalLabel = ordinalLabel
60	        self.formulaText = formulaText
61	        self.components = components
62	        self.shippedOn = shippedOn
63	        self.colorHex = colorHex
64	        self.isActive = isActive
65	        self.isImportedPlaceholder = isImportedPlaceholder
66	        self.archivedAt = archivedAt
67	        self.createdAt = createdAt
68	        self.updatedAt = updatedAt
69	    }
70	
71	    /// What the UI should render: `displayLabel` if non-empty, otherwise the ordinal fallback.
72	    var resolvedLabel: String {
73	        let trimmed = displayLabel.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
74	        return trimmed.isEmpty ? ordinalLabel : trimmed
75	    }
76	
77	    /// Default version palette (matches the JSX `VERSIONS` map: emerald → blue → violet → cyan).
78	    static let defaultPaletteHexes: [String] = [
79	        "#34D399",
80	        "#60A5FA",
81	        "#C084FC",
82	        "#67E8F9"
83	    ]
84	}

86	// MARK: - Intervention window (V3)
87	
88	/// Bounded period during which a `ProtocolFormulaVersion` was the active intervention.
89	/// One window per version. Closed when the next version ships (`endedAt = next.shippedOn`)
90	/// or when the version is archived (`endedAt = archivedAt`). Active version's window has
91	/// `endedAt = nil`. Used for sleep-data attribution and effect-size analysis.
92	nonisolated struct InterventionWindow: Codable, Hashable, Sendable, Identifiable {
93	    enum Phase: String, Codable, Hashable, Sendable, CaseIterable {
94	        /// Version is currently active (`endedAt == nil`).
95	        case active
96	        /// Closed because a newer version shipped.
97	        case superseded
98	        /// Closed because the version was archived without a successor.
99	        case archived
100	    }
101	
102	    var id: UUID
103	    var versionID: UUID
104	    var startedAt: Date
105	    var endedAt: Date?
106	    var phase: Phase
107	    var createdAt: Date
108	    var updatedAt: Date
109	
110	    init(
111	        id: UUID = UUID(),
112	        versionID: UUID,
113	        startedAt: Date,
114	        endedAt: Date? = nil,
115	        phase: Phase = .active,
116	        createdAt: Date = Date(),
117	        updatedAt: Date = Date()
118	    ) {
119	        self.id = id
120	        self.versionID = versionID
121	        self.startedAt = startedAt
122	        self.endedAt = endedAt
123	        self.phase = phase
124	        self.createdAt = createdAt
125	        self.updatedAt = updatedAt
126	    }
127	
128	    /// Inclusive-start, exclusive-end check against a sleep date.
129	    func contains(_ date: Date) -> Bool {
130	        guard date >= startedAt else { return false }
131	        if let endedAt { return date < endedAt }
132	        return true
133	    }
134	}

273	        stdRestorativeMin: Double?,

278	        continuityCategoryDistribution: [SleepContinuityCategory: Double],
279	        isInsufficient: Bool,

302	        self.stdRestorativePctOfInBed = stdRestorativePctOfInBed

305	        self.continuityCategoryDistribution = continuityCategoryDistribution
306	        self.isInsufficient = isInsufficient

308	        self.stdDeepMin = stdDeepMin
309	        self.meanRemMin = meanRemMin

316	        self.stdLatencyMin = stdLatencyMin
317	        self.meanSleepScore = meanSleepScore
318	        self.stdSleepScore = stdSleepScore
319	    }
320	
321	    var missingExtendedMetricLabels: [String] {
322	        var missing: [String] = []
323	        if meanDeepMin == nil { missing.append("Deep") }
324	        if meanRemMin == nil { missing.append("REM") }
325	        if meanAwakeMin == nil { missing.append("Awake") }
326	        if meanTotalSleepMin == nil { missing.append("Sleep duration") }
327	        if meanLatencyMin == nil { missing.append("Latency") }
328	        if meanSleepScore == nil { missing.append("Score") }
329	        return missing
330	    }

332	    var hasExtendedMetrics: Bool {
333	        missingExtendedMetricLabels.isEmpty
334	    }
335

337	        let missing = missingExtendedMetricLabels
338	        guard !missing.isEmpty else { return "complete" }
339	        return missing.joined(separator: ", ")
340	    }
341	}

425	/// Identifies a single Protocol Formula metric across snapshot, rollup, baseline, and
426	/// impact-summary structs. `betterIsLower` flips delta-color logic in views (awake +
427	/// latency are the lower-is-better axes; everything else is higher-is-better).
428	nonisolated enum ProtocolFormulaMetric: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable, Sendable {
429	    case restorativeMin
430	    case restorativePct
431	    case longestBlock
432	    case deep
433	    case rem
434	    case awake
435	    case duration
436	    case latency
437	    case score
438	
439	    var id: String { rawValue }
440	
441	    var betterIsLower: Bool {
442	        switch self {
443	        case .awake, .latency: true
444	        default: false
445	        }
446	    }
447	
448	    var shortLabel: String {
449	        switch self {
450	        case .restorativeMin: "Restore"
451	        case .restorativePct: "Rest %"
452	        case .longestBlock: "Block"
453	        case .deep: "Deep"
454	        case .rem: "REM"
455	        case .awake: "Awake"
456	        case .duration: "Sleep"
457	        case .latency: "Latency"
458	        case .score: "Score"
459	        }
460	    }
461	
462	    var fullLabel: String {
463	        switch self {
464	        case .restorativeMin: "Restorative Sleep"
465	        case .restorativePct: "Total Restorative Sleep %"
466	        case .longestBlock: "Longest Restorative Block"
467	        case .deep: "Deep Sleep"
468	        case .rem: "REM Sleep"
469	        case .awake: "Awake Time"
470	        case .duration: "Total Sleep"
471	        case .latency: "Sleep Latency"
472	        case .score: "Sleep Score"
473	        }
474	    }
475	
476	    var unit: String {
477	        switch self {
478	        case .restorativePct: "%"
479	        case .score: "pts"
480	        default: "min"
481	        }
482	    }
483	
484	    var colorHex: String {
485	        switch self {
486	        case .restorativeMin: "#34D399"
487	        case .restorativePct: "#60A5FA"
488	        case .longestBlock: "#C084FC"
489	        case .deep: "#818CF8"
490	        case .rem: "#F472B6"
491	        case .awake: "#FBBF24"
492	        case .duration: "#67E8F9"
493	        case .latency: "#FB923C"
494	        case .score: "#A3E635"
495	        }
496	    }
497	
498	    func value(from snapshot: ProtocolNightMetricSnapshot) -> Double? {
499	        switch self {
500	        case .restorativeMin: snapshot.restorativeSleepMinutes
501	        case .restorativePct: snapshot.restorativePctOfInBed
502	        case .longestBlock: snapshot.longestRestorativeBlockMinutes
503	        case .deep: snapshot.deepMinutes
504	        case .rem: snapshot.remMinutes
505	        case .awake: snapshot.awakeMinutes
506	        case .duration: snapshot.totalSleepMinutes
507	        case .latency: snapshot.latencyMinutes
508	        case .score: snapshot.sleepScore
509	        }
510	    }
511	
512	    func baselineValue(from baseline: ProtocolBaselineSnapshot) -> Double? {
513	        switch self {
514	        case .restorativeMin: baseline.meanRestorativeMin
515	        case .restorativePct: baseline.meanRestorativePctOfInBed
516	        case .longestBlock: baseline.meanLongestRestorativeBlockMin
517	        case .deep: baseline.meanDeepMin
518	        case .rem: baseline.meanRemMin
519	        case .awake: baseline.meanAwakeMin
520	        case .duration: baseline.meanTotalSleepMin
521	        case .latency: baseline.meanLatencyMin
522	        case .score: baseline.meanSleepScore
523	        }
524	    }
525	
526	    func rollupMean(from rollup: ProtocolVersionRollup) -> Double? {
527	        switch self {
528	        case .restorativeMin: rollup.meanRestorativeMin
529	        case .restorativePct: rollup.meanRestorativePctOfInBed
530	        case .longestBlock: rollup.meanLongestRestorativeBlockMin
531	        case .deep: rollup.meanDeepMin
532	        case .rem: rollup.meanRemMin
533	        case .awake: rollup.meanAwakeMin
534	        case .duration: rollup.meanTotalSleepMin
535	        case .latency: rollup.meanLatencyMin
536	        case .score: rollup.meanSleepScore
537	        }
538	    }
539	}

544	    case restorativeImprovement
545	    case restorativeRegression
546	    case longestBlockImprovement
547	    case continuityImprovement
548	    case lowData
549	    case baselineUnavailable
550	}
551	
552	nonisolated struct ProtocolFormulaInsight: Identifiable, Sendable {
553	    var id: UUID
554	    var kind: ProtocolFormulaInsightKind
555	    var versionID: UUID?
556	    /// Short headline shown in the card.
557	    var headline: String
558	    /// Supporting body (always includes the observed-not-causal caveat when applicable).
559	    var body: String
560	    var isPositive: Bool
561	
562	    init(id: UUID = UUID(), kind: ProtocolFormulaInsightKind, versionID: UUID? = nil,
563	         headline: String, body: String, isPositive: Bool = true) {
564	        self.id = id; self.kind = kind; self.versionID = versionID
565	        self.headline = headline; self.body = body; self.isPositive = isPositive
566	    }
567	}
568	
569	// MARK: - Hashing helper
570	
571	nonisolated enum ProtocolFormulaHashing {
572	    /// Stable SHA-256 of (normalized formula text + sorted component name|dose|role).
573	    /// Used by `ProtocolNightLog.formulaSnapshotHash` so logs detect drift against the version.
574	    static func snapshotHash(for version: ProtocolFormulaVersion) -> String {
575	        let text = version.formulaText.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
576	        let components = version.components
577	            .map { "\($0.name)|\($0.dose)|\($0.role.rawValue)" }
578	            .sorted()
579	            .joined(separator: ";")
580	        let combined = "\(text)::\(components)"
581	        let digest = SHA256.hash(data: Data(combined.utf8))
582	        return digest.map { String(format: "%02x", $0) }.joined()
583	    }
584	}
